name: Comedy Feature Film
description: Comedy-optimized Save the Cat structure with built-in timing for setups, punchlines, callbacks, running gags, and the Rule of Threes.

beats.name: Opening Image / Cold Open Gag
beats.description: A comedic snapshot that establishes tone. Often the protagonist's most embarrassing or absurd moment in their ordinary world. Sets up the 'normal' that comedy will subvert.
beats.purpose: Get the first laugh. Establish comedic tone immediately.

requiredElements.description: First joke/gag within 30 seconds
Protagonist shown in an absurd or relatable situation

beats.name: Theme Stated (Ironic Advice)
beats.description: Someone gives the protagonist advice they'll initially ignore. In comedy, this is often delivered ironically or the protagonist openly mocks it.
beats.purpose: Plant the theme the protagonist will learn the hard way.

requiredElements.description: Theme delivered in a funny way — protagonist dismisses it

beats.name: Set-Up (Comedy World Building)
beats.description: Establish the protagonist's comedic world. Introduce their flaws as comedic traits. Set up running gags (minimum 3). Introduce the ensemble cast with their quirks.
beats.purpose: Plant all comedic setups that will pay off later. Minimum 3 running gags established.

requiredElements.description: Introduce ensemble cast with distinct comedic traits
Establish at least 3 running gags
Joke density: at least 1 laugh every 30-45 seconds

beats.name: Catalyst (The Absurd Disruption)
beats.description: Something ridiculous happens that changes everything. In comedy, the inciting incident should be inherently funny or lead to an inherently funny situation.
beats.purpose: Launch the comedy premise. The 'what if' moment.

requiredElements.description: An absurd, funny event disrupts the protagonist's world

beats.name: Debate (Comedy of Errors Begins)
beats.description: The protagonist tries to avoid the new situation, creating escalating mishaps. Each attempt to restore normalcy makes things funnier/worse.
beats.purpose: Build comedic momentum through escalating failed attempts.

requiredElements.description: Series of escalating comedic mishaps
Funny dialogue exploring the protagonist's denial

beats.name: Break Into Two (All-In on Absurdity)
beats.description: The protagonist commits to the absurd situation, either willingly or because they have no choice. They dive into the comedy premise fully.
beats.purpose: End of setup — now we deliver on the premise. Maximum comedy begins.

requiredElements.description: Protagonist fully commits to the premise

beats.name: B Story (Heart of the Comedy)
beats.description: The relationship that grounds the comedy in genuine emotion. Usually a romance or deep friendship. This is where the movie has a 'heart'.
beats.purpose: Provide emotional depth. The audience cares because of this relationship.

requiredElements.description: The 'heart' character — love interest or genuine friend
Genuine connection moment amid the comedy

beats.name: Fun and Games (Peak Comedy)
beats.description: THE COMEDY SHOWCASE. Maximum joke density. Set pieces. Physical comedy. The trailer moments. All running gags hit their second beat (Rule of Threes).
beats.purpose: Deliver maximum laughs. This is the section the audience came for.

requiredElements.description: Major comedy set pieces (2-3 big sequences)
Running gags hit their 2nd beat (Rule of Threes)
Optional comedy montage showing escalation

beats.name: Midpoint (The Lie Works... Too Well)
beats.description: FALSE VICTORY: The protagonist's comedic scheme appears to succeed spectacularly. Everything is perfect... which means it's about to go very wrong.
beats.purpose: Peak of false success. The bigger the high, the funnier the fall.

requiredElements.description: The protagonist's plan succeeds beyond expectations
Hint that this success is built on shaky ground

beats.name: Bad Guys Close In (The Lie Unravels)
beats.description: The deception/scheme starts falling apart. Misunderstandings compound. The protagonist juggles increasingly impossible situations. Comedy of errors peaks.
beats.purpose: Escalating comedy of complications. Things go wrong in increasingly funny ways.

requiredElements.description: Escalating complications — each fix creates two new problems
Close calls where the truth almost comes out

beats.name: All Is Lost (The Truth Comes Out)
beats.description: The lie is exposed. The B-Story character discovers the deception. The protagonist loses everything they gained AND the genuine relationship they built.
beats.purpose: The big reveal. Comedy stops. The audience feels the weight of the lie.

requiredElements.description: The deception is fully exposed
Genuine hurt from the B-Story character

beats.name: Dark Night of the Soul (Lonely Without Laughs)
beats.description: The protagonist is alone. No more jokes. The comedy pauses for genuine emotional vulnerability. This is the scene that earns the movie its emotional credibility.
beats.purpose: Show the protagonist's genuine remorse. No comedy — pure heart.

requiredElements.description: Protagonist alone, genuinely vulnerable — no jokes

beats.name: Break Into Three (The Honest Approach)
beats.description: The protagonist realizes the truth they were told at the beginning. They decide to fix things the RIGHT way — through honesty, not deception.
beats.purpose: Comedy and heart merge. The protagonist earns their redemption through authenticity.

requiredElements.description: Protagonist understands the theme through their own experience

beats.name: Finale (Grand Gesture + Callbacks)
beats.description: The protagonist makes a grand, honest gesture to win back what they lost. ALL running gags hit their third beat. Callbacks to setups throughout the film pay off.
beats.purpose: Maximum satisfaction: emotional resolution + comedy payoffs. All setups pay off.

requiredElements.description: Grand gesture scene — public, honest, probably embarrassing
ALL running gags hit their 3rd beat (Rule of Threes completion)
B-Story character forgives through seeing genuine change

beats.name: Final Image (Tag Gag)
beats.description: Mirror of the opening, but the world is transformed. Often includes a final joke or callback. The new 'normal' is funnier and better than the old one.
beats.purpose: Last laugh. Visual proof of growth. Leave the audience smiling.

requiredElements.description: Final joke or callback that gets the last laugh

genreRules.rule: First laugh within 30 seconds of the film starting
Joke density: minimum 1 laugh per 30-45 seconds in comedic scenes
Rule of Threes: every running gag needs exactly 3 beats — setup, callback, payoff
The Dark Night of the Soul must have ZERO jokes — this earns emotional credibility
Callback spacing: at least 20-40 minutes between setup and first callback
Comedic escalation in sets of 3: Normal → Exaggerated → Absurd
Every character should have a distinct comedic voice/quirk
Physical comedy beats should escalate within each sequence
The finale must pay off ALL running gags and callbacks
End on a laugh, not just an emotion

mainPlot.name: The Scheme / Deception / Fish-Out-of-Water Situation
mainPlot.description: Protagonist pursues their goal through comedy-generating means (deception, disguise, culture clash, etc.)
mainPlot.thematicFunction: The external comedy reveals internal truths about the protagonist

subPlots.name: Heart Story (Love/Friendship)
subPlots.description: The genuine relationship the protagonist risks losing by maintaining the lie
subPlots.thematicFunction: Provides the emotional stakes that give the comedy meaning

subPlots.name: Sidekick Subplot
subPlots.description: The best friend/sidekick has their own comedic arc that mirrors or contrasts the main plot
subPlots.thematicFunction: Comic relief escalation and contrast to protagonist

phases.name: The Faker
phases.characterState: Uses charm/deception as a coping mechanism

phases.name: The Schemer
phases.characterState: Doubles down on the lie because it's working
phases.internalConflict: Growing attachment to B-story person vs. maintaining the scheme

phases.name: The Juggler
phases.characterState: Desperately trying to keep all plates spinning
phases.internalConflict: Knows the truth must come out but can't face it

phases.name: The Exposed
phases.internalConflict: Must face who they really are without the comedy armor
phases.externalManifest: Alone, vulnerable, no jokes to hide behind

phases.name: The Authentic
phases.characterState: Acts from genuine self for the first time
phases.internalConflict: Fear of rejection while being real
phases.externalManifest: Grand gesture that's honest, probably embarrassing, but real
